---
name: web-ui-ux-enhancer
description: "Use when improving website structure, information hierarchy, layout, responsiveness, spacing, typography, color, and overall UI/UX polish."
user-invocable: true
---

# Web UI/UX Enhancer

## When to Use
- The page feels cluttered, flat, or hard to scan.
- The content structure needs better hierarchy or flow.
- Layout, spacing, typography, or visual balance needs improvement.
- The experience should feel more polished on desktop and mobile.

## Procedure
1. Review the current page structure and identify the main user goal.
2. Map the content into clear sections with a stronger hierarchy.
3. Simplify the layout so the most important actions and messages stand out.
4. Improve visual design with better spacing, type scale, color contrast, and alignment.
5. Check responsiveness and adjust the layout for smaller screens.
6. Verify accessibility basics such as readable contrast, tap targets, and semantic structure.
7. Summarize the changes and note any remaining tradeoffs or follow-up work.

## Quality Checks
- The page has a clear primary action or focal point.
- Related content is grouped logically.
- Visual spacing makes the page easier to scan.
- Mobile layout remains readable and usable.
- The design looks intentional rather than default or crowded.
